data.title: Strathcona to Offer 0.62 Strathcona Share and C$4.10 in Cash Per MEG Share >SCR.T MEG.T
Japan's Economy Shrinks for the First Time in a Year — WSJ
data.description:
By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO — Japan's economy shrank for the first time in a year and faces a bumpy road ahead due to the impact of U.S. trade policy.

Japan's real gross domestic product declined 0.2% in the January-March period from the previous quarter, preliminary government data showed Friday, putting the risk of a technical recession in play.

That compared with economists' forecast for a 0.1% contraction and the 0.6% growth recorded in the October-December quarter. The economy shrank 0.7% on an annualized basis in the first quarter.

The GDP data came amid concerns that U.S. tariffs could hurt Japan's hard-earned economic recovery by weighing on exports and making companies pull back on investment.

"With the economy already shrinking on the eve of the trade war, the Bank of Japan will probably wait even longer before resuming its tightening cycle than we had anticipated," said Marcel Thieliant, head of Asia-Pacific at Capital Economics.

Thieliant said it has become increasingly difficult for the BOJ to raise interest rates in July as forecast.

Reflecting uncertainty about the global trade outlook, the BOJ recently halved its growth projection for the current fiscal year ending March 2026. While the central bank is still seeking further interest-rate increases, some economists say it might not be able to make any moves this year if trade frictions deepen.

In the first quarter, external demand, or exports minus imports, shaved 0.8 percentage point off growth, with exports down 0.6% and imports up 2.9%.

As U.S. tariffs hurt exports, one of Japan's main growth engines, persistent inflation has damaged another: private consumption, which accounts for about half of GDP. Friday's data showed that private spending was flat in the January-March period from the previous quarter.

"Continued price rises pose a downside risk to Japan's economy, affecting private consumption through a decline in consumer confidence," economy minister Ryosei Akazawa said at a news conference Friday.

Akazawa said tariff concerns haven't affected consumption yet, but the minister, who is in charge of trade negotiations with the U.S., promised to pay careful attention to consumers' mood after a recent government survey showed the possibility of worsening sentiment.

Solid corporate earnings have spurred companies to increase investments and offer pay raises. Capital expenditure was up 1.4% in the first three months of the year from the previous quarter.

Higher wages are expected to help the recovery in consumption, although food inflation has dimmed sentiment among households.

Credit Agricole economist Takuji Aida said the BOJ would need to confirm that the Japanese economy didn't deteriorate in the July-September period and update their forecasts in January before resuming rate increases.

Some economists, including Aida, expect the Japanese economy to shrink again this quarter, pushing the nation into a technical recession, defined as two consecutive quarters of contraction.

There is a chance that the second estimate of first-quarter GDP will show an upward revision, moving the economy out of the recession danger zone. That said, an upgrade isn't likely to skew the outlook too positively, said Stefan Angrick, head of Japan and frontier markets economics at Moody's Analytics.

"Coming data won't change the picture of an economy that's struggling," he said in a note.

How negotiations between Tokyo and Washington proceed is another factor to consider.

Japanese officials have been asking the Trump administration to reconsider the levies, but whether a deal will be reached remains unclear. The two sides have agreed to hold another round of discussion in mid-May or later.

So far, "2025 is stacking up to be a difficult year as tariff pain and weak domestic momentum threaten to stifle growth," said Angrick.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: French Real Estate Investor Icade Issues EUR500 Million Green Bond
data.description:
Icade issued a 10-year green bond worth 500 million euros, with an annual coupon of 4.375%, under its euro medium-term note program, according to a Thursday release.

The French real estate investment company will use the proceeds from the issue to finance or refinance assets and projects under its green financing framework.

Concurrently, the company also launched a tender offer for four existing series of notes to proactively manage its debt structure and extend financial maturities. The offer will close on May 21.

BNP Paribas, Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank, La Banque Postale, Natixis, and Société Générale were the active bookrunners for the issue.

data.title: New Jersey Transit rail strike to begin on Friday, union says
data.description:
New Jersey's commuter rail engineers will walk off the job early on Friday after 11th-hour contract talks with the third-largest U.S. public transit system stalled just ahead of a midnight deadline set for meeting their wage demands, the workers union said late on Thursday.

The first labor strike against the New Jersey Transit agency in more than 40 years was set to go into effect at 12:01 a.m. EDT (0401 GMT) on Friday, the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen said in a statement.

The union, which represents 450 NJ Transit engineers who drive the agency's commuter trains, said a marathon 15-hour bargaining session on Thursday broke off when management negotiators walked out of the talks at 10 p.m.

The union announcement came as New Jersey Governor Phil Murphy and NJ Transit's Chief Executive Officer Kris Kolluri were holding a news conference, telling reporters talks had "paused" but that management remained willing to return to the bargaining table at any time.

They also outlined the agency's contingency plans for dealing with a strike that is expected to idle trains serving hundreds of thousands of commuters in New Jersey and New York.

data.title: Strathcona Offer Represents Total Consideration of C$23.27 for Each MEG Share >SCR.T
Malaysia's First-Quarter Economic Growth Matches Initial Estimates
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong and Amanda Lee

KUALA LUMPUR--Malaysia's first-quarter economic growth slowed, confirming a weaker-than-usual start of the year.

Growth in the Southeast Asian economy was supported by continued strength in domestic demand, the central bank said Friday.

Household spending was sustained amid positive labor market conditions and income-related policy measures, including the upward revision of minimum wages and civil servant salaries.

A steady expansion in investment activity was driven by the realization of new and existing projects, the central bank said.

Export growth was slower due mainly to lower mining exports, it added. However, this was partially offset by stronger electrical and electronics exports and tourism, the central bank said.

That lifted Malaysia's gross domestic product in the first three months of 2025 by 4.4%, compared with the same period a year earlier, matching the advance estimate of 4.4% growth released in April, Bank Negara said in a statement.

On a quarter-on-quarter seasonally-adjusted basis, GDP growth expanded 0.7%.

The revised print confirms the first sub-5% expansion since the first quarter of 2024, coming ahead of the U.S.'s announcement of tariffs in April that spurred uncertainty about trade and economic stability.

A Wall Street Journal poll of economists had tipped revised first-quarter growth would come in at 4.5%.

Malaysia's growth this year will be affected by rising trade tensions and heightened policy uncertainties, the central bank added. Rapidly-evolving developments around trade tariffs are expected to affect the global outlook for the rest of the year, it added.

As a small and open economy, Malaysia will face direct and indirect impact from these tariffs, Bank Negara Malaysia Gov. Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said in a statement.

Economic growth this year is expected to be slightly lower than the earlier forecast of 4.5% to 5.5%, he said.

"The high uncertainty surrounding outcomes of trade negotiations and how these will reshape global trade complicates a clear assessment of their impact on growth at this juncture," he added.

The new official growth forecast will be released in the near future once there is a greater visibility in these factors, he said.

Headline inflation moderated to 1.5% in the first quarter, largely due to lower utilities inflation, the central bank said.

In the first quarter of the year, the ringgit remained broadly stable, it added.

External factors are expected to continue influencing the ringgit exchange rate, the central bank said.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com and Amanda Lee at amanda.lee@wsj.com

data.title: LHN's Attributable Profit Rises Nearly 9% in Fiscal H1
data.description:
LHN's attributable profit in fiscal H1 was up 8.8% to SG$14.1 million from SG$13.0 million a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Distribution per share or DPS remained unchanged at SG$0.01.

Revenue jumped 29% year over year to SG$70.6 million from SG$54.5 million, mainly due to increased revenue from facilities management segment.

The company declared a dividend of SG$0.01 per share for the period.

data.title: Canadian oil producer Strathcona to initiate takeover bid for MEG Energy
data.description:
Canadian oil and gas producer Strathcona said on Friday that it will initiate a takeover bid for MEG Energy Resources in a deal that values the Canadian energy firm at C$5.93 billion ($4.25 billion), according to Reuters calculations.

($1 = 1.3944 Canadian dollars)

data.title: Slump in Uranium Prices Drives Down Shares of Uranium Producers
data.description:
The stock prices of Australian uranium producers fell on the back of a slump in uranium prices.

Shares of Boss Energy fell 6% in recent trading on Friday, while those of Paladin Energy fell 8%.

data.title: Nvidia plans Shanghai research center, FT reports
data.description: Nvidia is seeking to build a research and development center in China, and the company's top boss Jensen Huang discussed the plan with Shanghai's mayor Gong Zheng when they met in April, the Financial Times reported on Friday.

data.title: Fitch Upgrades BAE Systems on Expected Boost to Profitability, Free Cash Flow
data.description:
Fitch upgraded on Thursday the long-term issuer default rating of BAE Systems (BA.L) to A- from BBB+, with a stable outlook.

The rating agency said the upgrade is based on expectations that the British aerospace and defense company's profitability and free cash flow will benefit from record order backlog and sustainable business profile improvements.

data.title: India's Bharti Airtel falls after report of stake sale by SingTel
data.description:
** Bharti Airtel down 2.6% to 1,819.5 rupees; among top losers on Nifty 50 , which is trading 0.25% lower

** Singapore Telecommunications expected to sell shares worth $1 billion in BRTI on the day through block deals, Business Standard newspaper reports, citing sources

** Southeast Asia's largest telecom operator, SingTel, looking to sell 47.6 mln Airtel shares at floor price of 1,800 rupees, a 3.6% discount to stock's last close, report adds

** STEL and BRTI did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comments

** CLSA says stake sale shouldn't come as surprise as it's part of STEL's efforts to equalise shareholding with the Mittal Group in BRTI

** YTD, BRTI gains 14.4%

data.title: Korea Electric Power's Net Attributable Income Jumps 314.7% in Q1
data.description:
Korea Electric Power posted first-quarter net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company of 2.328 trillion won, up 314.7% from 561.5 billion won a year earlier, according to a Wednesday regulatory filing with Korea Exchange.

The company's sales rose 4% year over year to 24.2 trillion won from 23.3 trillion won.

Shares of Korea Electric Power rose more than 1% in recent trade on Friday.

data.title: NJ Transit Workers Go On Strike: What to Know — WSJ
data.description:
By Esther Fung

New Jersey Transit workers are on strike, following a yearslong battle over wages.

Mark Wallace, national president of the Brotherhood of Locomotive Engineers and Trainmen, confirmed that the union and NJ Transit did not reach an agreement on a new labor contract Thursday night, so the strike began at 12:01 a.m. Hundreds of thousands of commuters could be impacted across New Jersey, New York and Philadelphia.

New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy said the union rejected a deal that gave engineers virtually identical wages to engineers working for the Long Island Rail Road without "blowing up" the agency's budget.

How can NJ Transit riders get to New York City during the strike?

NJ Transit said it has contingency plans that include bus services that substitute the rail lines, but estimated that these buses can only carry approximately 20% of current rail customers. It added that commuters can also seek alternatives from private carriers, and the Metro North Railroad. Some passengers might also consider taking the NY Waterway ferry.

The Metro-North Railroad will extend its services that includes a network of cross-Hudson routes and honor parking permits at select stations and west-of-Hudson tickets, the Metropolitan Transportation Authority said. It will also add train cars to certain routes where ridership is expected to increase.

What could happen next?

The strike could snarl commutes starting Friday morning and into next week for more than 300,000 people across the region. Any trains still operating at 12:01 a.m. will finish their route. There will be no further trains in operation and NJ Transit said it will provide buses for some of the train routes. NJ Transit has already been telling commuters to find alternative transportation, or to work from home if possible. NJ Transit said it will stop service for Shakira's concerts at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford on Thursday and Friday.

What is NJ Transit and the union fighting over?

They disagree over the size of the wage increases for a contract that would cover around 450 engineers from July 2020 to June 2028.

Engineers said NJ Transit's proposed wage increases are not equitable to what their counterparts at Long Island Rail Road make. BLET General Chairman Thomas Haas said its latest proposal is only $4 million more than NJ Transit's most recent offer.

NJ Transit Chief Executive Officer Kris Kolluri said the number is larger because BLET's proposal would result in requests for similar wage increases in the 14 other labor unions that have already agreed to new contracts. Fares would have to increase, costing taxpayers and NJ Transit an additional $684 million between 2025 and 2030, the transportation company said.

How much does a NJ Transit locomotive engineer make?

The average annual wage of a locomotive engineer in 2024 was around $135,400, including overtime, Kolluri said. BLET's Haas has disputed this, saying it is actually $113,000.

The highest paid locomotive engineer made $224,000 last year, while the lowest paid engineer made $58,172, according to a list of individual wages of each engineer in 2024 provided by NJ Transit. Wages vary over the number of hours worked and their seniority.

What was included in the March tentative agreement?

NJ Transit engineers, who haven't had a wage increase since 2020, rejected a tentative labor agreement in March. Under the agreement, workers would get a 2% to 4% annual increases through June 30, 2028 as well as a retroactive lump sum payment starting from July 2020. NJ Transit said that represents a 27.9% increase over the nine-year period.

Could the government intervene?

Under the Railway Labor Act, Congress can make both sides accept an agreement that their members have voted down.

In 2022, then-President Joe Biden signed legislation to avert a national freight rail shutdown, imposing a contract that four out of 12 railroad unions had rejected.

This time though, it is smaller passenger rail that has a limited impact on the country's economy so there is little appetite from Congress to step in.

This explanatory article may be periodically updated.

Write to Esther Fung at esther.fung@wsj.com

data.title: Fitch puts Taiwan life insurers on downgrade watch after currency surge
data.description:
Credit ratings agency Fitch on Thursday placed five Taiwanese life insurers under review for potential downgrades after a sharp surge in the Taiwanese dollar this month put stress on their balance sheets.

Fitch put Cathay Life Insurance, Fubon Life Insurance, KGI Life Insurance, Nan Shan Life Insurance and Taiwan Life Insurance on "Rating Watch Negative" due to the "substantial currency mismatch" produced by their "sizeable" U.S. dollar holdings, the ratings agency said in a press release.

"While insurers have hedged a majority of their balance sheet mismatches, we believe this strategy will come under pressure due to the surge in hedging costs, and unhedged positions continue to expose them to sharp currency swings," Fitch said.

"The potential for further Taiwan dollar appreciation remains."

Taiwan's dollar experienced an unprecedented 8% two-day surge at the start of this month in what analysts postulate was a scramble to repatriate U.S.-based investments, with confidence in the U.S. dollar tarnished by President Donald Trump's trade war, and as speculation built that U.S.-Taiwan tariff negotiations might include an agreement to weaken the greenback.

Fitch estimates the insurers have sufficient capital buffers to withstand a 10% rise in the Taiwan dollar against the U.S. dollar from the start of 2025. Currently, the local currency is up about 8.8% this year.

The ratings agency said it expects to resolve the reviews over the next three to six months.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singtel to Sell $1 Billion Worth of Shares in Bharti Airtel
data.description:
Singtel is expected to dispose of shares around $1 billion in Bharti Airtel through block deals, Business Standard reported Thursday, citing sources.

The Singaporean telco plans to sell around 47.6 million shares at 1,800 Indian rupees per share, which represents a 3.6% discount on the stock's latest closing price, the report noted.

data.title: Rio Tinto Invests $1.2 Billion to Upgrade Canadian Hydro Plant
data.description:
Rio Tinto Group is making a $1.2 billion investment to update its Isle-Maligne hydroelectric facility in Quebec, Canada.

The work includes replacing eight turbine-alternator groups, upgrading their water intake and hydraulic passage, replacing electrical and mechanical equipment, modifying a spillway, and constructing an extension and mechanical workshop, the mining giant said Thursday.

The project is slated to run until 2032 to guarantee the future of low-carbon aluminum production in Saguenay-Lac-Saint-Jean.

data.title: Partners Group Portfolio Company Green Tea Debuts on Hong Kong Bourse
data.description:
Partners Group said Friday its portfolio company Green Tea Group commenced trading the same day on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Green Tea, an operator of restaurants serving fusion cuisine, started trading under the ticker 6831 following an initial public offering of 168,364,000 common shares at HK$7.19 apiece.

The Swiss private equity company owned 15.76% of Green Tea when the latter debuted.

data.title: Strathcona Resources Plans to Acquire MEG Energy Shares It Doesn't Already Own >SCR.T MEG.T
Prudential Prices SG$600 Million Worth of Bonds Due 2035
data.description:
Prudential said its unit Prudential Funding (Asia) priced SG$600 million worth of 3.800% subordinated bonds due May 22, 2035.

The bonds are part of the issuer's $10 billion medium term bond program, according to a Thursday bourse disclosure.

Prudential will use the proceeds for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Spring Airlines' Passenger Capacity Increases 12% in April
data.description:
Spring Airlines' passenger capacity jumped 12% year over year to 4.92 billion available seat kilometers in April, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The Chinese airline company's passenger traffic rose 9.3% year on year to 4.34 billion revenue passenger kilometers during the same month.

Moreover, the company's cargo and mail capacity climbed 16% to 7,240.78 tons from the previous year, the filing said.

data.title: Sunpower Group Co-Founder Resigns as CEO
data.description:
Sunpower Group said Chief Executive Officer Ma Ming has resigned, effective May 15, citing personal reasons.

Ma, a co-founder of the company, has served as an executive director since 2004 and was named CEO in 2021. He played a key role in steering the group through its IPO in Singapore.

The provider of industrial steam and civil heating did not name a successor to Ma in its Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

data.title: India's Policybazaar-owner PB Fintech rises on quarterly profit surge
data.description:
** Shares of India's PB Fintech rise nearly 2% to 1,812 rupees

** Stock set to gain for the sixth straight session, if trend holds

** Parent of insurance marketplace Policybazaar reported nearly 3x jump in Q4 profit after tax to 1.71 billion rupees ($20 million), while rev rose 38%

** Stock, on an avg, rated "hold" with median PT 1604 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Day's move trims YTD losses to ~16%

($1 = 85.3900 Indian rupees)

data.title: Lithium Royalty Corp. Announces Final Results Of Substantial Issuer Bid
data.description:

  • LITHIUM ROYALTY CORP. ANNOUNCES FINAL RESULTS OF SUBSTANTIAL ISSUER BID

data.title: Gecina Signs Preliminary Deal to Buy Paris Office Complex for EUR435 Million
data.description:
Gecina signed a preliminary deal to buy a two-building office complex in Paris' central business district for 435 million euros.

The real estate company said Thursday that it plans to reposition the recently restructured 32,200-square-meter property into a business center with amenities.

Gecina expects the cost of the refurbishment plan, to be carried out in 12 to 15 months, to range between 30 million euros and 40 million euros.

data.title: Imugene to Seek Shareholder Approval for Capital Consolidation
data.description:
Imugene said it will seek shareholder approval in June for a 34-to-1 share consolidation, reducing its share count from 7.47 billion to about 219.6 million, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company said the move is expected to lift the stock's trading price.

Shares of the company fell 2% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: China Southern Airlines climbs as April passenger numbers rise
data.description:
** HK shares of China Southern Airlines rise 5.8% to HK$3.67, highest since March 31

** Stock set for biggest one-day pct rise since May 12

** Shanghai-listed stock climbs 0.7% to 5.94 yuan

** Chinese carrier says its April passengers carried rises 9.1% y/y; mail and cargo carried up 7.4% y/y

** YTD, HK stock loses 15%, Shanghai shares fall 8.6%

data.title: Indian benchmarks open marginally lower dragged by IT, financials
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes inched lower at the open on Friday, a day after hitting seven-month highs on progress in trade negotiations with the United States.

The Nifty 50 fell 0.23% to 25,000.8 and the BSE Sensex lost 0.29% to 82,281.95 as of 9:24 a.m. IST.

Seven of the 13 major sectors logged losses. A pullback in information technology stocks and financials after a sharp rally earlier in the week pushed the benchmarks to the red.

Both benchmarks hit seven-month highs on Thursday. The Nifty and the BSE Sensex have gained about 4.5% and 4%, respectively, in the last four sessions.

The India-Pakistan border truce initiated a relief rally on Monday, while progress in a trade deal with the U.S. firmed gains in the previous session.

Other Asian markets were muted as a rally triggered by the U.S.-China trade truce hit a pause.

The MSCI Asia ex-Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) traded flat on Friday, after closing little changed in the previous session.

($1 = 85.4050 Indian rupees)

data.title: Malaysia's economy grows 4.4% in Q1
data.description:
Malaysia's economy grew 4.4% in the first quarter of 2025 from a year earlier, below market expectations but in line with government estimates, data showed on Friday.

Economists surveyed by Reuters had forecast annual gross domestic product growth would come in at 4.5% in the January to March period.

On a quarter-on-quarter seasonally adjusted-basis, first quarter GDP expanded 0.7% vs a 0.2% contraction in the previous quarter.

The economy grew a revised 4.9% in the final quarter of 2024.

The central bank said first quarter economic growth was driven by sustained household spending growth amid positive labour market conditions and policy support, as well as steady expansion in investments, and continued export growth.

"Growth was affected by lower oil and gas production, and normalisation in motor vehicle sales and production," Bank Negara Malaysia Governor Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said at a press conference.

Growth in 2025 is expected to be slightly lower than the 4.5 to 5.5% forecast range, Abdul Rasheed said, adding a new estimate would be announced in the next month or two.

"The balance of risk to the growth outlook is currently tilted to the downside," Abdul Rasheed added.

Last month, the central bank said it will have to lower this year's forecast economic growth range of 4.5% to 5.5% due to the global trade war.

Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim said earlier this month that the suspension of most of the U.S. tariffs until July meant the economic impact was manageable for now, but Malaysia was unlikely to meet its growth forecast this year.

Malaysia is facing a 24% tariff rate on its exports to the U.S. from July, unless it is able to negotiate a reduction of the levy.
